feed.title: Accessibility Wars - hammer
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: Venom [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to use a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. Condition damage and eight-legged pets are empowered through the trait Arachnophobia. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
Ritualist's Trapper
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Ritualist's
Amulet Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
Accessory Ritualist's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Ritualist's
Ring Ritualist's
Relic Fractal
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Dagger Ritualist's Malice
Torch Ritualist's Doom
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Axe Ritualist's Malice
Dagger Ritualist's Geomancy

Consumables

  •  Salsa-Topped Veggie Flatbread
  •  Magnanimous Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQQeHSE3SB95AHkAvwClAb0AAAC6ABsADxvtAAYkKwEAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADBQAvAGYAAA==]
Juvenile Carrion Devourer
Juvenile Black Widow Spider

Rotation

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation
  4. Repeat

Precast

  1. Sharpening Stone

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. All your Utility Skills (7/8/9)
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst (Essential Unleashed Pet F1)
    • Rending Vines (Optional F2)
    • Enveloping Haze (Optional F3)
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you just have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleashed Ambush to proceed with the quickness rotation.
  • The 3 pet skills have no cast time but venomous outburst is your biggest contributor. The other 2 may be skipped to lower your APM if they slow your rotation. The damage difference is negligible.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, can just use F2.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Neurotoxin Burst OR
    • Sundering Volley
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation - L to R (A/D) R to L (D/T)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> use the skills from left to right for Axe/Dagger and from right to left for Dagger/Torch. The first skill you use will be recharged faster. This skill is bolded in the rotation and should be cast twice before Unleash Pet and reentering the opener. This will maximise the effect of Quick Draw. This is bolded in the rotation below.

  • Dagger/Torch 5432 then 5
    1. Skill 5
    2. Skill 4
    3. Skill 3
    4. Skill 2
    5. Skill 5
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Axe/Dagger 2345 then 2
    1. Skill 2
    2. Skill 3
    3. Skill 4
    4. Skill 5
    5. Skill 2
    6. Pet F2 (if available)
    7. Continue with Opener (see above)
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired.
  • Any condition based spider or devourer pet is preferred due to Arachnophobia
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Using Unleash Ranger too early will lead to a large DPS loss. Using it late will lead to a small DPS loss and maintain quickness.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Tail Lash (F1)
  2. Exploding Spores (Utility Alternative)
  3. Spike Trap (Utility Alternative)
  4. Bring other pets if you need more

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Whirling Bunny Thumper [qDPS]
entry.content:
Notes

This build is simplified to activate the  Relic of the Claw to reduce relic downtime on burst windows on a fixed, consistent and easy-to-remember rotation. This provides steady quickness for the group and decent sustained CC. There is a relatively a large amount of button downtime filled with auto attacks. This build however still performs decently and allows one to comfortably learn the class mechanics with fewer APMs while providing very good and steady quickness uptime.

There are two channeled skills that will destroy your build when cancelled. These skills are positioned in the rotation where they are less likely to be cancelled (because the other skills are on cooldown).

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
Berserker's Fireworks
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Claw
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Berserker's Air
Hydromancy
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Sword Berserker's Air
Axe Berserker's Hydromancy

Consumables

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQQgHx45SBd5AHkAARsAAKUBAACsAQAADxsAAEAQARMAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADMwBaAAUAAA==]
Juvenile Black Moa
Juvenile Wallow

Rotation

Start in Unleash Pet using Sword/Axe

General Rotation

The entirety of quickness Untamed rotation can be summarised into two steps.

  1. Opener (optional)
  2. Quickness Rotation
  3. DPS Rotation

Opener

The opener can give a very good burst, especially when the boss has different phases.

  1. Unleash Pet
  2. "Sic 'Em!"
  3. 3 pet skills (can be cast simultaneously)
    • Venomous Outburst
    • Rending Vines
    • Enveloping Haze
  4. Unleash Ranger
  • If you skip the opener, you have to use Unleash Ranger to unlock Unleash Ambush to give quickness.
  • Starting the opener with sword will trigger  Relic of the Claw right at the beginning which is a significant initial DPS burst.
  • If you use a 2KRO/3KRO keyboard, your simultaneous cast of pet skills may result in input failure. Most gaming keyboards are 6KRO, allowing all the buttons to be pressed at the same time. Alternatively, you press the three pet skills one by one as fast as you can.

Quickness Rotation

This build starts off with a very simple quickness rotation through the trait Unleashed Power.

  1. Unleashed Ambush
    • Relentless Whirl OR
    • Deft Strike
  2. 🔃 Weapon Swap
  3. Unleashed Ambush (other weapon)
  4. Continue with DPS Rotation

DPS Rotation (2/3/4/5/7/8)

After using your 2nd Unleash Ambush -> Autos about to start/started

  1. Weapon 2
    • Unleashed Wild Swing OR Pounce
  2. Weapon 3
    • Unleashed Overbearing Smash OR Serpent's Strike
  3. Weapon 4
    • Unleashed Savage Shock Wave OR Path of Scars
  4. Weapon 5
    • Unleashed Thump OR Whirling Defense
  5. Utilities 7/8 if available +/- Pet skill (F3)
    • Exploding Spores OR "Sic 'Em!"
    • Undead Plague
  6. Optional Weapon 2 on refresh
  7. Autos (till 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes)
  8. Unleash Pet -> Unleash Ranger
  9. Continue with Quickness Rotation
  • Use "Sic 'Em!" on cooldown or during a comfortable window when you are not pressing any skills.
  • Your pet skills don't interrupt your rotation and you may use them as desired. Any power based pet is viable.
  • Try not to cancel Relentless Whirl or Whirling Defense.
  • You may wish to introduce a slight delay after casting Relentless Whirl
  • The sequence of button presses are similar on both weapons.
  • If you are slow on your rotation, you can skip Weapon 3.
  • If you use Unleash Ranger before your 🔃 Weapon Swap refreshes, your Unleashed Ambush will not be ready.
  • Dragging your feet on using ambush will lead to a small 1-2k DPS loss. Swapping Unleash Mode too early will lead to a higher DPS loss.

Relic of the Claw Uptime

< Relic of the Claw is triggered by your CC skills. These are littered between your important skill casts except during the end of the hammer phase. If you have followed the DPS rotation above Exploding Spores should be available towards the later part of your hammer DPS rotation.

If you started off with the wrong weapon, then remember to reserve Exploding Spores for hammer.

Crowd Control Skills

  1. Deft Strike before Relentless Whirl
  2. Path of Scars before Whirling Defense
  3. Exploding Spores during hammer rotation
  4. Your pet F3 skills deal CC through the trait Beastly Warden

Benchmark

This bench was done with ascended food, standard utiltiies and without infusions.

entry.title: Gyroscopic Hammer [pDPS]
entry.content:
Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Berserker's Force
Air
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Thief

Consumables:

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Superior Sharpening Stone

Build

[&DQMGNyYvKz3ZEioPJgGGAK4SowCGAAAAgxISAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADWgBXAFUAAA==]

Notes

Use off cooldown:

  1. Shredder Gyro
  2. Thunderclap (Hammer 5)
  3. Electro-whirl (Hammer 2)
  4. Grenade Barrage
  5. Throw Mine
  6. Mine Field (press this twice)

You want to try to maintain as much superspeed as possible as that increases your DPS. You can do this by using Medic Gyro opposite to Shredder Gyro, when Shredder Gyro has 10s of cooldown left. You can also press it before you press any high damage skill if you forgot to use it before, but generally this ensures you have a high uptime of superspeed, increasing your damage.

Crowd Control

Your CC skills are:

  1. Spare Capacitor
  2. Throw Mine
  3. Mine Field
  4. Thunderclap (Hammer 5)

You can also use Function Gyro if you have allies that have been downed, and this will summon up to 3 seperate Gyros, which will automatically go revive allies nearby.

Video

entry.title: Big Bang Mech [pDPS]
entry.content:
Signet-heavy, with some chill hammer thumping."

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Berserker's Force
Impact
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
Accessory Berserker's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Thief
  • Consumables:
    • Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak (Power/Ferocity)
    • Superior Sharpening Stone (Power)

Build

[&DQMGOyYvRh4NGyoPJgGGABobowAQGwAACRsSAQAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADMwBaAFcAAA==]

Notes

To play this build, you want to place

  • Throw Mine

at the start of the fight, then press

  1. Thunderclap (Hammer 5)
  2. Electro-whirl (Hammer 2)
  3. Overclock Signet
  4. Throw Mine

off cooldown, and that is basically your rotation.

Crowd Control

You have decent CC. Primarily, Rocket Charge (Hammer 3) will make your Mech do Rocket Punch, which is a CC, but do take note that since the cooldown of Rocket Punch is longer than that of Rocket Charge it will not always be available.

Other CC skills that you have are:

  • Throw Mine
  • Force Signet
  • Overclock Signet

Video

entry.title: Symbolic Healbender [aHeal]
entry.content:
This is a very accessible build that requires minimal effort to play, where majority of its effects are based on auto-attacks. The end of the auto-attack chain Symbol of Protection rely on Writ of Persistence to provide heals. You may find that DPS is fairly decent (for a healer). Ideally, the build should function as a low-toughness healer.

This build is low cognition, low physical complexity, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content. This build requires only one weapon with no requirement of a weapon swap as long as the partial fury requirement is met. This means there are certain quick DPS builds (such as Deadeye, Berserker, Conduit, Ritualist, Harbinger, Untamed, Chronomancer, Troubadour and more) that are ideal to pair with this healer. Alternatively, a simple occasional spear rotation and/or utility adjustments to cover fury.

Gearing

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Harrier's Transference
Concentration
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Harrier's Transference
Concentration
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Karakosa

Budget Options

  • Replace  Superior Rune of the Monk with  Superior Rune of Rebirth
  • Replace  Superior Sigil of Transference with  Superior Sigil of Water

Note: Weapon swap is optional, all the available options will lock you out of hammer and thus drop your boon coverage. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Bountiful Maintenance Oil

Build

Heal Alacrity Build

[&DQExLS4tQRp/ADgBLQEtATYBSAEyATIBcRIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAABMwAA]

Trait Alternatives

  • Bring Master of Consecrations if you are taking consecrations.
  • Bring Protector's Restoration if there aren't blockable mechanics.

This build is a simplified version of the heal alacrity build on snowcrows with selective preferences for lower intensity. If you are able to adapt to the version on SC, feel free to follow their guide.

Rotation and Utilities

Heal Alac Rotation

  1. Use your virtues starting from Rushing Justice (F1) -> Flowing Resolve (F2) -> Crashing Courage (F3).
  2. Auto Attack with minimal interruptions, using "Advance!" can provide the group with permanent swiftness without interrupting your autos.
  3. Use Flowing Resolve (F2) on cooldown, afterwards use Rushing Justice (F1)/ Crashing Courage (F3) to reposition.
  4. Use "Stand Your Ground!" when you require stability.
  5. Burst Heal Spear rotation for Fury (Refer to Burst Heal Section)

The importance of your auto attacks cannot be emphasized enough. Your auto attacks heal, provide alacrity, regeneration, might and protection and a field to trigger Relic of Karakosa with! You may skip the spear rotation and take "Feel My Wrath!" instead. Some boon DPS classes provide additional fury uptime, freeing your spear rotation for burst healing.

Burst Heals and Revives

  1. Use Glacial Blow (Hammer 2) for burst heals (due to Relic of Karakosa). You may refresh this skill using Banish (Hammer 4).
  2. Use "Receive the Light!" for a burst heal.
  3. Use Signet of Mercy as an emergency revive.
  4. Swap to spear ->5,4,3,2,1:
    1. Symbol of Luminance(Spear 5)
    2. Solar Storm(Spear 4)
    3. Gleaming Disc(Spear 3)
    4. Helio Rush(Spear 2)
    5. Daybreaking Slash(Spear 1 Auto Attack)
    6. Swap back to hammer when ready

Crowd Control rotation

  1. You have decent CC by just using Banish (Hammer 4) and Zealot's Embrace (Hammer 3).
  2. You can extend your CC rotation by starting with Glacial Blow (Hammer 2) and refreshing it with Banish (Hammer 4).
  3. If you need more CC you may bring Sanctuary, which is ideally used in a precast scenario so you can top it up with Hammer 4.

Hybrid DPS

  1. This build does decent DPS.
  2. For low heal-pressure fights, you may bring more power traits to increase your DPS.

Other tips and tricks

  • You may drop "Stand Your Ground!" for different utilities.
  • Crashing Courage allows you to shadow step through mechanics
  • Blocking increases your heal power and doing so intelligently allows efficient use of Invigorated Bulwark

Optimisation

Raid Ready

  1. W1 Gorseval - Immobilise your spirit with Signet of Wrath and Zealot's Embrace (Hammer 3)
  2. W1 Sabetha - You can teleport through flame wall with Crashing Courage.
  3. W2 Slothasor - Bring Absolute Resolve for additional condition cleanses.
  4. W2 Matthias - Bring Wall of Reflection for reflects. Bring Sanctuary if you require more CC.
  5. W3 Escort - This build is able to solo towers albeit a little slow with 6-8k dps. If you want to do the JP, bring "Feel My Wrath!" for a superspeed boost.
  6. W4 Samarog - You may knock Rigom back with Banish (Hammer 4) and immobilise with Zealot's Embrace (Hammer 3) and Chains of Light (Scepter 3). Bring Sanctuary if you require more CC.
  7. W4 Deimos - If you are the healer, due to the low toughness, you will probably be tasked to provide group aegis for the other DPS players.
  8. W6 Conjured Amalgamate - Your power DPS can be quite significant despite being a healer. Do not stop attacking with autos. Place Sanctuary when CA tosses out shields for a precast CC.
  9. W6 Qadim - You want to precast Sanctuary for each CC phase and load the CC bar with Banish (Hammer 4) as soon as the minibosses appear.
  10. W7 Sabir - For the final phase if your group needs projectile blocks, consider Shield of the Avenger and Wall of Reflection

With the new reduced cooldown, Mace/Focus can be an alternate weapon to tank, e.g. to block Deimos comfortably. However there are much better healing/tank options out there.

entry.title: Symbolic Dragonhammer [pDPS]
entry.content:
The purpose of writing this guide is to provide an ultra low intensity method to play PvE dragonhunter while easily hitting the 70% benchmark with minimal effort. This build is low cognition, low physical complexity, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content. The utilities do not require specific timing and do not interrupt the auto attack nature of the build. This build requires only one weapon to deal damage to near competitive levels with no requirement of a weapon swap: Hammer!

Gearing and Consumables

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
Berserker's Scholar
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Hammer Assassin's Force
Accuracy
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Berserker's
Amulet Marauder
Accessory Marauder
Accessory Assassin's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Berserker's
Ring Berserker's
Relic Mistburn

Infusions

 Precise WvW Infusion (optional)

Weapon Swap

Weapon swap is optional, all the available options will massively reduce DPS and lock you out of hammer, they should be prioritised based on utility requirements.

  1. Bow - Bow provides a ranged option for bosses with melee DPS downtime.
  2. Scepter/Focus - Provides a decent immobilise option with some ranged DPS.

Optimised Berserker/Marauder/Assassin's Loadout GW2Skills Link

This requires 1 +5 precise infusion, power infusions are optional

Food

  •  Cilantro Lime Sous-Vide Steak
  •  Plate of Spicy Moa Wings

Utility

  •  Superior Sharpening Stone(Power)

Build

Power DPS

[&DQEqOjEtGys4AQAAhBIAAP4SAAD+AAAAcRIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=]

Trait Alternatives

There is a Core Guardian variant which provides more selfless support but due to the large amounts of gear differences (crit requirement at 40% only), it will not be covered in this guide. Due to recent changes to the virtues trait, the core guardian build has issues maintaining its own resolution but can still work with certain healers. The build has hence been removed from our library. This is the only viable hammer auto-attack build at the time of writing, underperforms and has a higher APM. Dragonhunter provides a low-repetition option and is therefore the build of choice on this site.

Rotation and Utilities

DPS Rotation

  1. Precasts - Dragon's Maw (elite)
  2. Opening - Banish (Hammer 4)
  3. Use Test of Faith and Fragments of Faith on cooldown. You may try to time these when Relic of the Brawler is active.

Dragon's Maw precasts may not be possible on some fights although it is the best option for DPS. Bring Signet of Courage instead for passive healing. If you are not familiar with the fight, Signet of Courage is also the preferred default elite skill.

Crowd Control rotation

  1. You have decent CC by just using Banish (Hammer 4) and Zealot's Embrace (Hammer 3).
  2. You can precast Dragon's Maw (elite) for additional CC just before the CC phase of an encounter.

Sustainability

  1. You may opt to take Signet of Courage for passive healing.
  2. Your autos generate perma protection and your symbols heal you.
  3. Smite Condition for condi cleanse
  4. "Stand Your Ground!" or Fragments of Faith provides group stability and a stun break
  5. Fragments of Faith provides Aegis

Other Utilities and extra style points

You may drop Smite Condition for different utilities.

  • Judge's Intervention allows you to shadow step through mechanics
  • You can also pair this while casting Ring of Warding for a Teleport CC
  • You get stability from Heavy Light just by using your CC skills.

Optimisation

Cleave

The dragonhunter variant has a larger cleave radius than its core counterpart. The pull, power cleave and trap combo is extremely useful when taking down adds.

A few examples where this advantage can be used are:

  1. Harvest Temple - Any phase with adds.
  2. W2 Slothasor and its slublings, F1 is a targeted pull that can avoid pulling allies. The cleave effect can take down slublings fast!
  3. Fighting adds on W3 towers.
  4. W3 Keep Contruct and the Adds
  5. W6 Sword and Shields

Raid Ready

  1. W1 Gorseval - Immobilise your spirit with Signet of Wrath and Zealot's Embrace (Hammer 3)
  2. W2 Bandit Trio - The self sustainability and high amounts of CC allow this build to do mortars very efficiently.
  3. W3 escort - For backwarg, bring Signet of Wrath which gives a generous 6s immobilise. You have other skills that can immobilise and cripple too!
  4. W6 Qadim - You may choose to use this build for lamp instead if 2 players are sent to do Lamp. Bring stability for the third lamp.

Caution: Leftover traps on the arena can trigger mechanics e.g.

  • ball mechanic on KC
  • killing deadly adds like tormented dead

Golem Tips

  1. Dragon's Maw will start the encounter early.
  2. Signet of Courage is brought for the benchmark instead.
  3. Ideally you want to frontload the damage using Banish (Hammer 4) because it has a long precast and a short aftercast.
